#### **Target audience: Students who do not drink water often.**

#### **Step 1: The "Brain" (Microcontroller)**

You need a tiny computer to run the game.

* **Solution:** Use an Arduino Nano or a **Raspberry Pi**. These are small, cheap boards that can control sensors and screens.  
* **The Code:** You’ll program a simple "If/Then" logic: *If* 1 hour passes without drinking, *Then* the speaker will say “ Please drink your water”.

#### **Step 2: The "Eyes" (The Screen)**

You need a way to see the Penguin.

* **Solution:** A small **OLED Display** (about 1 inch big).  
* **The Art:** You can draw 3 simple pictures: an **Egg**, a **Chick**, and a **Big Blue Penguin**. The "Brain" will switch between these pictures based on the water level.


#### **Step 3: The "Scale" (Weight Sensor)**

How does the bottle know you drank?

* **Solution:** A **Load Cell** (Weight Sensor) placed at the very bottom of the bottle.  
* **The Logic:** When the weight drops (e.g., from 500g to 400g), the "Brain" tells the Penguin on the screen to grow.

#### **Step 4: The "Nudge" & "Sleep" (Vibration & Switch)**

* **The Buzz:** Connect a **Mini Vibration Motor** (like the ones in old pagers).  
* **The Switch:** Add a simple **Toggle Switch**. When flipped to "Off," it cuts the power to the motor so the Penguin (and child) can sleep.  
* **The Reset:** Program the "Brain" so that every morning (or when the battery is turned back on), the Penguin starts back at **Stage 1: The Blue Egg**.

#### **Step 5: The "Shell" (The Bottle Case)**

* **Solution:** You don't need to build a whole bottle\! You can 3D-print or build a **"Smart Base"** that a regular water bottle sits inside of. This keeps the electronics safe and dry.

**Step 6: The Speaker**

 What if they just ignore the buzz or not see it?

* **Solution:** Add a speaker so that whenever it buzz, it will say: "Remember to drink more water\!**”**

**Step 7: In-case-of-exam switch**

What if they have an exam and the water bottle buzzes and says: “Remember to drink more water\!” during the exam?

* **Solution:** an in-case-of-exam switch: if we flick it so that it goes to “on”, it will change the vibrating and talking into a printed message on the OLED display that says; Remember to drink more water\!”   
  **How to do the printing on the display, type:**  
  **print(‘Remember to drink more water\!’)**

---

### **📊 The Project Logic Flow (How it runs)**

1. **Morning:** Child turns the switch **ON**. ➔ **Screen:** Blue Egg.  
2. **Noon:** Child hasn't drank for 1 hour. ➔ **Action:** Bottle vibrates.  
3. **Afternoon:** Child drinks half the bottle. ➔ **Screen:** Penguin Chick.  
4. **Evening:** Bottle is empty\! ➔ **Screen:** Big Blue Penguin (Mission Complete).  
5. **Night:** Child turns switch **OFF**. ➔ **Screen:** Sleeping Penguin.  
6. **Next Day:** Turn **ON**. ➔ **Screen:** Resets to Blue Egg.

![][image1]

---

* **Hardware Needed:** Raspberry PI (Brain), OLED (Screen), Load Cell (Scale), Vibration Motor (Buzzer), Speaker. 

![][image2]

### **Hardware Procurement List: Penguin Smart Bottle (Portable Prototype)**

| Component | Purpose | Recommended Model | Approx. Price (SGD) | Purchase Link (Singapore/Fast Shipping) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **The Brain** | Runs your Python logic | **Raspberry Pi Pico 2 (with Headers)** | $8.00 – $10.00 | [element14 SG](https://sg.element14.com/raspberry-pi/raspberry-pi-pico-2/mcu-board-rpi-pico-2-520kb-150mhz/dp/4531086)OK  |
| **The Screen** | Displays the Penguin pet | **0.96" OLED (I2C Interface)** | $8.85 – $12.00 | [PolyMate Tech](https://www.polymate.tech/shop/elec-modu-ole-01t-oled-display-096-i2c-module-73)OK |
| **The Scale** | Measures water weight | **1kg Load Cell  \+ HX711 Kit** | $9.50 – $14.00 | [Shopee SG](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://shopee.sg/1Kg-5kg-10Kg-Kit-Load-Cell-with-HX711-Amplifier-Weighing-Scale-IOT-DIY-i.969092529.25729393786&authuser=1)OK |
|  |  |  |  |  |
| **The Battery** | Portable power source | **3.7V LiPo Battery (1000mAh)** | $8.00 – $12.00 | [SGBotic LiPo](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://www.sgbotic.com/index.php%3Fdispatch%3Dproducts.view%26product_id%3D2232&authuser=1)OK |
| **The Charger** | Recharges battery via USB | **TP4056**  **Charging Module** | $1.50 – $3.00 | [Shopee SG](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://shopee.sg/search%3Fkeyword%3DTP4056%2520module&authuser=1)OK |
| **Connection** | Connects parts (No-solder) | **Breadboard & Jumper Wires** | $5.00 – $8.00 | [Amicus SG](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://amicus.com.sg/products/breadboard-400-tie-point/&authuser=1)OK |
|  |  |  |  |  |
| **EST. TOTAL** | **Full Portable Setup** |  | \*\*$46.35 – $69.00\*\* |  |

**Why not Raspberry PI 5 ?**

### **1\. Cost (The Biggest Difference)**

* **Raspberry Pi Pico 2:** Costs about **$7.50 SGD**.  
* **Raspberry Pi 5:** Currently costs between **$65.00 – $205.00 SGD** due to a global memory shortage in 2026\.  
* *Verdict:* You can buy **10-20 Picos** for the price of one Pi 5\. 

### **2\. Power Consumption (Battery Life)**

* **Pico:** Uses a tiny amount of electricity. **It can run on a small battery** for **days or weeks**.  
* **Pi 5:** Is a full computer that needs a wall plug or a massive power bank. It generates a lot of heat and usually needs a fan.  
* *Verdict:* A water bottle needs to be portable. A Pi 5 would make the bottle heavy, hot, and the battery would die in a few hours.

### **3\. Startup Speed**

* **Pico:** Turns on **instantly** (less than 1 second). As soon as you flip the switch, your Python code starts running.  
* **Pi 5:** Has to "boot up" like a laptop (loading Linux, checking files). It takes **20–40 seconds** just to start.  
* *Verdict:* Nobody wants to wait 30 seconds for their water bottle to "load" before they can see their pet penguin.

### **4\. Hardware Interaction**

* **Pico:** Designed specifically to talk to sensors (like your Load Cell) and screens. It has built-in **Analog-to-Digital Converters (ADC)** which are essential for many sensors.  
* **Pi 5:** Is designed for high-speed tasks like AI, web browsing, and 4K video. It actually lacks some of the simple "hardware-friendly" features the Pico has.

Workflow:  
Full bottle: Little egg  
Two thirds: Chick  
One third: Big Penguin

QUESTIONS:

1) How to use Raspberry Pi Pico? Does it support Python or other programming languages? C   
2) How to wire them up?   
3) How to display an image or animation on the OLED? Is it black and white or colourful?  
4) How to use the weight sensor?

Tasks:

1) Design three images or gifs for different states  
2) Write python code to process the signal and display on the screen.(github)  
3) Integration to a water bottle bag  
4) Dynamically support different water bottles.
